title: Hugging Face and Google Cloud Partner to Boost AI Efficiency | Julien Chaumond posted on the topic | LinkedIn
text:
Julien Chaumond
5mo
Report this post
I am super excited to announce that
Hugging Face
and
Google Cloud
are joining forces 🔥 

to make AI faster, safer & cheaper for all 🎉

Every day, over 1,500 terabytes of open models and datasets are downloaded and uploaded between Hugging Face and Google Cloud by millions of AI builders. We estimate that it generates over a billion dollars of cloud spend annually already.

So we’re excited to announce today a new partnership that will make it even faster, safer & cheaper for Google Cloud customers to work with Hugging Face. 

In this partnership, we will:
- reduce Hugging Face model & dataset upload and download times through Vertex AI and Google Kubernetes Engine thanks to a new gateway for Hugging Face repositories that will cache directly on Google Cloud
- offer native support for TPUs on all open models sourced through Hugging Face
- provide a safer experience through Google Cloud’s built-in security capabilities (including the great
VirusTotal
).

Ultimately, our intuition is that the majority of cloud spend will be AI related and based on open-source (rather than proprietary APIs) as all technology builders will become AI builders. 

And both Google Cloud and Hugging Face will be there for it, let's go!

Dawid Jozwiak
5mo
Report this comment
The cached models and TPU integration are smart tactics, but let's be real about what this actually is. Once your open models flow through Google's gateway with their security stack baked in, you're not running open source anymore. You're running Google's distribution of open source. The code stays free but the path to production runs through their infrastructure.

The billion dollar annual spend number gives away the game here. That's not organic adoption of open models. That's users who initially chose open source to avoid lock-in now paying cloud bills that exceed what they'd spend on API calls. The openness was always the gateway drug.

I'm not saying this is evil. It's actually pragmatic. But the framing as "making AI cheaper for everyone" misses that we're watching the industrialization of open source. Hugging Face gets distribution, Google gets the traffic and the data patterns, and builders get to feel like they're using something free while their actual costs climb.

The real test: will those billion dollars flow to open source communities or straight to Google's margin.
